title: Postal Service Mail Carriers
description: Sort and deliver mail for the United States Postal Service (USPS). Deliver mail on established route by vehicle or on foot. Includes postal service mail carriers employed by USPS contractors.

tasks.text: Scan labels on letters or parcels to confirm receipt.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Obtain signed receipts for registered, certified, and insured mail, collect associated charges, and complete any necessary paperwork.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Return to the post office with mail collected from homes, businesses, and public mailboxes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sort mail for delivery, arranging it in delivery sequence.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Deliver mail to residences and business establishments along specified routes by walking or driving, using a combination of satchels, carts, cars, and small trucks.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Meet schedules for the collection and return of mail.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sign for cash-on-delivery and registered mail before leaving the post office.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Hold mail for customers who are away from delivery locations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Turn in money and receipts collected along mail routes.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Leave notices telling patrons where to collect mail that could not be delivered.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain accurate records of deliveries.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Bundle mail in preparation for delivery or transportation to relay boxes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record address changes and redirect mail for those addresses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Return incorrectly addressed mail to senders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Answer customers' questions about postal services and regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide customers with change of address cards and other forms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Report any unusual circumstances concerning mail delivery, including the condition of street letter boxes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Register, certify, and insure parcels and letters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Travel to post offices to pick up the mail for routes or pick up mail from postal relay boxes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sell stamps and money orders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Complete forms that notify publishers of address changes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
